interactions.summary: He treats engagement and retention as the primary design objective, and then interprets a suicide-support failure case as an expected product of that objective structure.
He contends AI firms may frame China as an existential competitor for attention while simultaneously using Chinese data infrastructure, treating public threat narratives as partly strategic.
He argues the business model is not yet profitable on direct AI products, pointing to rapidly expanding capital spending and a turn toward state-backed surveillance infrastructure.

speaker_notes.note: The suicide incident appears as a read example from CNN; it is used rhetorically by Jiang and may mix quoted report text with his inference.

claims.claim: He defines the core product objective as maximizing user liking and usage ('intensity and engagement') over truth-preserving interaction quality.
He claims that American AI firms pair U.S.-led media narratives of Chinese danger with behind-the-scenes U.S.-China AI cooperation, as part of data and surveillance capture.
He asserts that large AI firms are in an escalation loop of spending and that state funding/surveillance use is the likely route to sustained scale.
He introduces ‘Stargate’ as a framing device for data-center-led strategy, linking model scale and infrastructure concentration to state surveillance capability.

signature_moments.source_phrase: The point of ChatGPT is to get you to like it
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This line extends the earlier model critique into institutional incentive structure and supports later control framing.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: An objective that prioritizes sustained user attention and repeat use over epistemic accuracy.

chronology_notes.note: This packet completes the move from abstract model-structure failures to empirical business/geopolitical mechanisms and prefaces the occult framing in later packets.

uncertainty_notes.note: The phrasing around 'in two ways' and several references is rhetorically compressed and may omit intermediate steps; treated as inferred causal model by the speaker.
